2606.26267.title: Accelerating Skill Assessment in Chess: A Drift-Diffusion-Enhanced Elo Rating System
2606.26267.paper_summary: Rating systems such as Elo serve as the gold standard for matchmaking in competitive chess. However, they inherently suffer from response lag due to their exclusive reliance on match outcomes, neglecting the granular quality of gameplay. Nevertheless, incorporating move-by-move information into rating adjustments presents a significant challenge given the substantial noise and the vastness of the game-state space. To address this, we propose the Drift-Diffusion-Enhanced Elo Rating System (DD-Elo), a novel skill assessment framework inspired by the drift diffusion model (DDM) from cognitive neuroscience. By modeling skill expression as a decision-making process, our model integrates move-level data to capture rapid skill fluctuations. We provide a rigorous mathematical derivation proving that DD-Elo maintains a bounded deviation from the traditional Elo system, ensuring theoretical alignment. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DD-Elo adapts to skill changes faster than Elo. Our findings suggest that DD-Elo offers an explainable, highly responsive, and backward-compatible solution for chess rating ecosystems. The implementation code is publicly available at https://github.com/Aquila-zhou1/DD-Elo .
2606.26267.paper_summary_zh: 評分系統如Elo作為競技棋類配對的黃金標準。然而，它們固有地受到反應延遲的影響，因為它們僅依賴比賽結果，忽略了遊戲過程的細微品質。儘管如此，將逐步移動信息納入評分調整中仍然是一個重大挑戰，因為遊戲狀態空間的噪音和廣度都相當龐大。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了漂移擴散增強Elo評分系統（DD-Elo），這是一個受到認知神經科學中的漂移擴散模型（DDM）啟發的新型技能評估框架。通過將技能表現建模為決策過程，我們的模型整合了逐步數據，以捕捉快速的技能波動。我們提供了嚴謹的數學推導，證明DD-Elo與傳統Elo系統保持有界偏差，確保理論上的一致性。廣泛的實驗表明，DD-Elo對技能變化的適應速度快於Elo。我們的研究結果表明，DD-Elo為棋類評分生態系統提供了一個可解釋、高度響應且向後兼容的解決方案。實施代碼可在 https://github.com/Aquila-zhou1/DD-Elo 獲得。
2606.26267.author: Tianyuan Zhou et.al.
2606.26267.authors: Tianyuan Zhou, Zhizheng Fu, Tianming Yang
